title: Strengthening Communities of Recovery Act
plain_summary: This bill changes the name of a program that helps communities take care of people who have problems with drugs or alcohol. The program gives money to communities so they can provide better support and services for people in recovery. The bill increases the amount of money the program gets each year from five million dollars to sixteen million dollars. This extra funding will last for five years starting in 2025. The goal is to help more communities build stronger programs to help people stay healthy and drug-free.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Strengthening Communities of Recovery Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Strengthening Communities of Recovery
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called Section 547 of the Public Health Service Act. The changes are three small updates. First, in the title of that section, the word building is replaced with the word strengthening. Second, in one part of the law that talks about what communities can do, it adds the words or strengthen after the word build. This means communities can either build new programs or make existing programs stronger. Third, the section changes how much money is set aside for this program. Instead of five million dollars for each year from 2019 through 2023, the new amount will be sixteen million dollars for each year from 2025 through 2029.
